channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Harvard College Announces Graduate Student Assistant Opportunity for First-Year Academic Programs in 2026
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Harvard College has officially opened applications for the Graduate Student Assistant position under the First-Year Academic Programs office for 2026. The opportunity provides Harvard graduate students with valuable hands-on experience in higher education administration, student programming, logistics coordination, and academic operations while earning competitive hourly compensation. The position is based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and is […]

The post Harvard College Announces Graduate Student Assistant Opportunity for First-Year Academic Programs in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Harvard College has officially opened applications for the Graduate Student Assistant position under the First-Year Academic Programs office for 2026. The opportunity provides Harvard graduate students with valuable hands-on experience in higher education administration, student programming, logistics coordination, and academic operations while earning competitive hourly compensation.

The position is based in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and is connected to Harvard College’s First-Year Academic Programs office, which plays a major role in supporting students during their transition into college life. According to the official announcement, selected candidates will work closely with the Rising Scholars Program and other academic initiatives designed to improve student engagement, orientation, and academic success.

The priority application deadline for the role is May 29, 2026, although Harvard noted that applications may continue to be reviewed until the position is filled.

About the Harvard First-Year Academic Programs Office

The First-Year Academic Programs office at Harvard College is responsible for managing and supporting the pre-concentration academic experience for undergraduate students. The office helps organize major student programs such as:

  • Student orientation
  • Academic onboarding
  • Visits programs
  • First-year support initiatives
  • Residential academic programming

One of the office’s flagship programs is the Rising Scholars Program (RSP), a seven-week residential academic experience designed for selected incoming first-year students.

The program combines:

  1. Interdisciplinary coursework
  2. Residential learning experiences
  3. Academic mentorship
  4. Community-building activities
  5. College transition support

For the 2026 cycle, the Rising Scholars Program will run from June 20 through August 8, 2026.

Overview of the Graduate Student Assistant Role

The Graduate Student Assistant will report directly to the Assistant Dean for First-Year Academic Programs while also collaborating closely with the Assistant Director of Administration.

The role provides graduate students with an opportunity to:

  • Gain practical higher education administration experience
  • Develop leadership and communication skills
  • Participate in student-centered programming
  • Build project management capabilities
  • Work alongside Harvard administrative teams

Harvard explained that the selected candidate will be deeply involved in logistics, communications, operations, and programming support for the Rising Scholars Program and other first-year initiatives.

Main Responsibilities of the Position

The Graduate Student Assistant will handle a wide range of administrative and operational tasks.

Key Duties Include:

1. Administrative and Program Support

  • Supporting the Assistant Dean’s portfolio of projects
  • Assisting with orientation and onboarding initiatives
  • Managing logistics for academic programming

2. Program Coordination

  • Coordinating student arrival preparation
  • Creating name tags and information packets
  • Preparing event materials and swag bags
  • Organizing scheduling and logistics

3. Communication Management

  • Managing the Rising Scholars inbox
  • Drafting and editing newsletters
  • Preparing correspondence and communications materials

4. Financial and Procurement Tracking

  • Organizing receipts and spending records
  • Assisting with procurement and disbursement processes
  • Supporting budget and inventory management

5. Collaboration and Stakeholder Engagement

  • Working with campus partners and vendors
  • Coordinating with multiple departments
  • Supporting event planning and execution

Harvard emphasized that the position requires someone who can multitask effectively while handling confidential information with professionalism and discretion.

Qualifications and Skills Required

Applicants must meet several essential requirements to be considered for the position.

Basic Qualifications

Candidates must:

  • Be currently enrolled as a full-time graduate student at a Harvard school
  • Have administrative experience
  • Be proficient in Microsoft Office tools including Excel, Outlook, Word, and PowerPoint
  • Have experience using Adobe Acrobat and Zoom

Additional Preferred Skills

Harvard is looking for candidates who demonstrate:

  • Program management experience
  • Strong customer service skills
  • Excellent written and verbal communication
  • Strong organizational abilities
  • Superb attention to detail
  • Flexibility and adaptability
  • Positive teamwork skills
  • Strong problem-solving capabilities

The institution also noted that ideal applicants should be solution-driven individuals who can work both independently and collaboratively.

Work Schedule and Compensation

The Graduate Student Assistant role offers flexible but demanding work expectations depending on the time of year.

Schedule Details

  • During the academic year: 15–20 hours per week
  • During summer: Up to 35 hours per week
  • Occasional nights and weekends may be required

Compensation

  • Hourly pay rate: $30 per hour

The role will be based on Harvard’s campus in Cambridge, Massachusetts.

Harvard also mentioned that hybrid work arrangements may be possible after the orientation period depending on scheduling agreements between the employee and supervisor.

Application Requirements

To apply, candidates must submit:

  1. A resume
  2. A cover letter (maximum two pages)

The cover letter should specifically address:

  • Experience managing multiple collaborative projects
  • Examples of solving knowledge or skills gaps
  • Ability to identify and use resources effectively

Applications and supporting materials should be submitted directly to:

Dawn Ling
Email: dawn_ling@fas.harvard.edu

Important Deadline

The priority application deadline is:

May 29, 2026

Harvard stated that applications may continue to be reviewed until the role is filled, but early application is strongly encouraged.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This Graduate Student Assistant role provides a unique opportunity for Harvard graduate students interested in:

  • Higher education administration
  • Academic operations
  • Student affairs
  • Program management
  • Leadership development

The position allows candidates to gain real-world administrative experience within one of the world’s most prestigious academic institutions while contributing directly to student success initiatives.

Graduate students seeking careers in education leadership, university administration, nonprofit management, or academic programming can particularly benefit from the experience and professional exposure offered through this role.

Before final selection, candidates may also be asked to complete a technical skills assessment related to Microsoft Office applications and administrative responsibilities.

Click here to find more information about this opportunity

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post Harvard College Announces Graduate Student Assistant Opportunity for First-Year Academic Programs in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of British Columbia Opens Applications for Fully Funded Postdoctoral Research Fellow Position in Canada 2026
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The University of British Columbia has announced applications for the 2026 Postdoctoral Research Fellow opportunity at its Vancouver campus in Canada. The prestigious research opportunity is hosted under the Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences and offers qualified researchers an opportunity to work in one of the world’s leading academic and innovation-driven institutions. The fellowship comes with […]

The post University of British Columbia Opens Applications for Fully Funded Postdoctoral Research Fellow Position in Canada 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of British Columbia has announced applications for the 2026 Postdoctoral Research Fellow opportunity at its Vancouver campus in Canada. The prestigious research opportunity is hosted under the Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences and offers qualified researchers an opportunity to work in one of the world’s leading academic and innovation-driven institutions.

The fellowship comes with an attractive annual salary ranging between CAD $70,000 and $90,000 plus additional employee benefits, making it one of the highly competitive postdoctoral opportunities currently open for international researchers. According to the official posting, applications will remain open until May 28, 2026.

The position is designed for highly motivated researchers seeking to advance their careers in Health Economics and interdisciplinary pharmaceutical sciences research. Successful applicants will join a globally respected academic environment known for cutting-edge innovation, diversity, and impactful scientific contributions.

About the University of British Columbia (UBC)

The University of British Columbia is consistently ranked among the top public universities globally and is internationally recognized for excellence in teaching, learning, and research. Located in Vancouver, British Columbia, UBC attracts scholars and researchers from across the world.

UBC has established itself as a major center for innovation in medicine, health sciences, artificial intelligence, public health, biotechnology, and pharmaceutical sciences. The institution strongly emphasizes diversity, equity, and inclusion in both its hiring and academic culture.

The university’s postdoctoral programs are specifically structured to help researchers:

  • Strengthen their publication records
  • Expand professional networks
  • Gain advanced research experience
  • Build competitive academic and industry careers
  • Access mentorship and interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities

Details of the Postdoctoral Research Fellow Position

The 2026 Postdoctoral Research Fellow position will be based within the Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences under the supervision of the Zhang Lab led by Wei Zhang.

Key Information

  • Institution: University of British Columbia (UBC)
  • Location: Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada
  • Department: Faculty of Pharmaceutical Sciences
  • Position Type: Full-time Postdoctoral Research Fellowship
  • Duration: 1-year term with possibility of renewal
  • Expected Start Date: September 1, 2026
  • Salary Range: CAD $70,000 – $90,000 annually plus benefits
  • Application Deadline: May 28, 2026
  • Job Requisition ID: JR24287

The university noted that the appointment may be renewed depending on funding availability, research performance, and institutional requirements.

Research Focus and Responsibilities

The selected fellow will contribute to advanced research initiatives in Health Economics and pharmaceutical sciences. Although the posting focuses on Health Economics, UBC’s broader research ecosystem encourages interdisciplinary collaboration involving:

  • Public health research
  • Health policy analysis
  • Pharmaceutical outcomes research
  • Data-driven healthcare innovation
  • Economic modeling and evaluation
  • Policy translation and implementation science

Postdoctoral fellows at UBC are often involved in:

  1. Publishing research papers in peer-reviewed journals
  2. Participating in grant proposal development
  3. Supervising graduate and undergraduate students
  4. Collaborating with international researchers
  5. Contributing to high-impact scientific discoveries

The fellowship environment is designed to support professional development and long-term academic growth.

Benefits of the Fellowship

This opportunity offers several major advantages for international researchers and early-career academics.

Financial Benefits

  • Annual salary between CAD $70,000 and $90,000
  • Employee benefits package
  • Access to university resources and facilities

Academic Benefits

  • Opportunity to work at a globally ranked university
  • Access to world-class research infrastructure
  • Collaboration with internationally recognized researchers
  • Publication and conference opportunities
  • Career mentorship and professional development

Career Development Benefits

  • Enhanced academic CV and research profile
  • Increased competitiveness for faculty and industry roles
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaborations
  • Networking with global experts in pharmaceutical sciences and health economics

UBC also provides structured support systems for postdoctoral fellows, including workshops, leadership development programs, and research training initiatives.

Who Should Apply?

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • Recent PhD graduates
  • Health economists
  • Pharmaceutical sciences researchers
  • Public health experts
  • Biostatisticians
  • Policy researchers
  • Researchers interested in interdisciplinary healthcare innovation

Applicants with strong analytical, quantitative, and research publication experience are particularly encouraged to apply.

International applicants are welcome, and UBC maintains a strong commitment to inclusive hiring and workforce diversity. The university encourages applications from underrepresented and marginalized communities globally.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must submit their applications through the official UBC careers portal before the application deadline.

Applicants are typically expected to prepare:

  • Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Cover letter
  • Academic transcripts
  • Research statement
  • Publication list
  • Reference letters or referee contacts

Candidates should ensure that all application materials are professionally prepared and tailored to the fellowship requirements.

Applications close on May 28, 2026. Late applications may not be considered.

For full details and application submission, applicants can visit the official UBC job portal.

The fellowship represents a significant opportunity for researchers aiming to build international academic careers while contributing to impactful healthcare and pharmaceutical sciences research in Canada.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post University of British Columbia Opens Applications for Fully Funded Postdoctoral Research Fellow Position in Canada 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations University Junior Research Consultant Jobs 2026 in Portugal: Entry-Level UN Careers in Digital Governance and Public Sector Innovation
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The United Nations University (UNU) has officially opened applications for two Junior Research Consultant positions at UNU-EGOV in Guimarães, Portugal. These exciting international opportunities are ideal for early-career professionals passionate about digital governance, public sector innovation, policy research, and digital transformation. The positions offer a unique opportunity to work directly within the United Nations system […]

The post United Nations University Junior Research Consultant Jobs 2026 in Portugal: Entry-Level UN Careers in Digital Governance and Public Sector Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations University (UNU) has officially opened applications for two Junior Research Consultant positions at UNU-EGOV in Guimarães, Portugal. These exciting international opportunities are ideal for early-career professionals passionate about digital governance, public sector innovation, policy research, and digital transformation.

The positions offer a unique opportunity to work directly within the United Nations system while contributing to impactful global projects that support governments and public institutions in improving governance systems through technology and innovation.

Successful candidates will join a dynamic international and multicultural environment where they will gain practical experience in consultancy, policy advisory, research, stakeholder engagement, project coordination, communications, and public sector reform initiatives.

Applications close on May 28, 2026.

About the United Nations University (UNU)

The United Nations University (UNU) serves as the academic and research arm of the United Nations system. It functions as a global think tank and postgraduate teaching organization that conducts policy-relevant research, capacity development, and knowledge-sharing initiatives aimed at addressing major global challenges.

For over five decades, UNU has contributed to solving critical global issues related to:

  • Sustainable development
  • Human security
  • Economic development
  • Governance
  • Digital transformation
  • Climate resilience
  • Public policy
  • Social inclusion

UNU collaborates closely with governments, UN agencies, academic institutions, and development organizations worldwide to advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

About UNU-EGOV

The positions are based at the United Nations University Operating Unit on Policy-Driven Electronic Governance (UNU-EGOV), located in Guimarães, Portugal.

UNU-EGOV specializes in supporting governments and public institutions through:

  • Digital transformation initiatives
  • Electronic governance systems
  • Public sector modernization
  • ICT policy implementation
  • Governance innovation
  • Digital public infrastructure
  • Capacity-building programmes

The institute combines research, consultancy, policy analysis, and advisory services to help governments improve governance systems through strategic applications of digital technologies.

About the Junior Research Consultant Positions

UNU-EGOV is recruiting two Junior Research Consultants to support its growing portfolio of projects related to:

  • Digital governance
  • Public sector reform
  • Digital transformation
  • Public innovation
  • Advisory and consultancy services
  • Applied policy research
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • International development projects

These positions are designed for motivated early-career professionals who want to build experience in international policy, governance, consultancy, and UN-related work.

The role offers practical exposure to real-world public sector challenges while enabling candidates to contribute directly to impactful advisory and research projects.

Why This Opportunity Is Important

Many early-career professionals struggle to find opportunities that combine:

  • International exposure
  • Meaningful policy work
  • Research experience
  • Advisory experience
  • UN system engagement
  • Professional growth

This opportunity addresses all these areas simultaneously.

The positions are particularly valuable because they emphasize learning-by-doing within an international environment while allowing candidates to contribute to high-impact projects involving governments and global institutions.

Main Responsibilities

Selected candidates will support a wide range of activities within UNU-EGOV.

Key duties include:

  1. Supporting consultancy and advisory projects related to digital transformation and governance
  2. Conducting desk research and literature reviews
  3. Collecting, organizing, and analyzing information from multiple sources
  4. Supporting stakeholder interviews and engagement processes
  5. Assisting with project proposals and policy-oriented deliverables
  6. Drafting reports, presentations, and analytical documents
  7. Proofreading and quality assurance of written outputs
  8. Supporting conferences, workshops, seminars, and expert meetings
  9. Assisting with project monitoring and communications
  10. Maintaining databases and information systems
  11. Supporting capacity-building and knowledge-sharing activities
  12. Contributing to policy papers and case studies

The role is dynamic and suited for individuals who enjoy working across multiple tasks and projects.

What Type of Candidate Is UNU Looking For?

UNU is seeking candidates who are:

  • Curious and eager to learn
  • Reliable and proactive
  • Comfortable with multitasking
  • Interested in both research and consultancy
  • Strong communicators
  • Adaptable in multicultural environments
  • Detail-oriented
  • Motivated to grow professionally

The organization specifically highlights that this role is not repetitive or narrowly focused. Instead, it is designed for professionals who want to rapidly develop skills and take on increasing responsibilities over time.

Educational Qualifications

Applicants must possess:

  • A Master’s degree in a relevant field such as:
    • Public Policy
    • Public Administration
    • Political Science
    • Governance
    • Digital Governance
    • Development Studies
    • Law
    • Economics
    • Information Systems
    • ICT
    • Related disciplines

A PhD is considered an added advantage but is not mandatory.

Required Skills and Experience

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Interest in digital transformation and public sector innovation
  • Strong English communication skills
  • Analytical and drafting abilities
  • Organizational skills
  • Ability to work under multiple deadlines
  • Interest in consultancy and advisory work
  • Commitment to producing high-quality outputs

Additional advantages include:

  • 1–2 years of professional experience
  • Experience with policy analysis
  • Exposure to international organizations
  • Research or event coordination experience
  • Government or public sector exposure

Language Requirements

Required:

  • Fluency in English

Advantageous but optional:

  • Portuguese
  • Spanish
  • French
  • Arabic

Salary and Benefits

The remuneration package ranges from:

EUR 13,200 to EUR 18,000 per year (before tax)

The exact salary depends on qualifications and experience.

Additional benefits include:

  • Full-time international work experience
  • Opportunities for professional growth
  • Exposure to UN projects and global governance work
  • Multicultural work environment
  • Potential participation in international travel missions
  • Networking opportunities within the UN system

Contract Duration

The positions are offered under a Consultant Contract (CTC).

Important details:

  • Initial duration: 1 year
  • Renewable contract possible
  • Full-time schedule (35 hours per week)
  • Hybrid/in-person arrangement
  • At least 3 days per week in Guimarães, Portugal

UNU notes that consultant contracts do not include paid annual leave or sick leave.

Work Location

Selected candidates will work at:

Guimarães, Braga, Portugal

The work arrangement combines onsite collaboration with flexibility.

Travel Opportunities

The role may include mission travel for project-related activities.

When travel is required, UNU covers:

  • Economy class flights
  • Accommodation
  • Meals and incidentals (within UN rates)

However, consultants are responsible for:

  • Visa costs
  • Insurance
  • Medical clearance
  • Security clearance requirements

Travel expenses will be reimbursed based on economy-class airfare, irrespective of the travel duration. Expenses related to accommodation, meals, and incidental costs must remain within the Daily Subsistence Allowance (DSA) rates established by the International Civil Service Commission (ICSC) at ICSC Official Website.

Application Process

Interested applicants must:

  1. Apply through the official UNU application portal
  2. Complete the UNU P11 Form
  3. Submit a motivation statement
  4. Provide references
  5. Answer position-specific application questions

UNU advises candidates not to use forms from other UN agencies and to carefully follow application instructions.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply is:

May 28, 2026

Applications must be submitted before 11:59 WEST. Late submissions will not be accepted.

Click HERE to apply.

Important Notes for Applicants

Visa Sponsorship

UNU does not sponsor work visas for this position. Candidates should carefully assess their eligibility to work in Portugal before applying.

Diversity and Inclusion

UNU strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Candidates from developing countries
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Diverse national and cultural backgrounds

Scam Warning

UNU does not charge application or recruitment fees. Applicants should be cautious of fraudulent recruitment schemes.

Why You Should Apply

This opportunity is particularly valuable for early-career professionals seeking:

  • International policy experience
  • UN system exposure
  • Governance and digital policy expertise
  • Applied consultancy experience
  • Professional growth
  • Global networking opportunities
  • Practical advisory skills

It offers a rare combination of policy, research, consultancy, and project implementation experience within a globally respected institution.

Learn more about the United Nations University Junior Research Consultant Jobs HERE.

Conclusion

The UNU Junior Research Consultant positions in Portugal provide an outstanding opportunity for emerging professionals interested in governance, policy, digital transformation, and public sector innovation.

Working within the United Nations system allows candidates to contribute directly to projects that shape governance systems and public service delivery globally while building critical professional skills in an international environment.

For ambitious graduates and early-career professionals looking to launch or strengthen an international policy career, this opportunity offers both meaningful impact and long-term professional development.

Discover more global Job, Research, and United Nations opportunities on OFY.

The post United Nations University Junior Research Consultant Jobs 2026 in Portugal: Entry-Level UN Careers in Digital Governance and Public Sector Innovation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Yale Ventures Investor Network Role at Yale University 2026: Hybrid Full-Time Opportunity in Startup Ecosystems, Venture Capital, and Innovation Strategy
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Working at Yale University means becoming part of one of the world’s most respected academic and research institutions while contributing to innovation, entrepreneurship, and the future of groundbreaking ventures. Yale is now offering an exciting professional opportunity through Yale Ventures for talented professionals interested in venture ecosystems, fundraising strategy, investor relations, startup operations, and innovation […]

The post Yale Ventures Investor Network Role at Yale University 2026: Hybrid Full-Time Opportunity in Startup Ecosystems, Venture Capital, and Innovation Strategy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Working at Yale University means becoming part of one of the world’s most respected academic and research institutions while contributing to innovation, entrepreneurship, and the future of groundbreaking ventures. Yale is now offering an exciting professional opportunity through Yale Ventures for talented professionals interested in venture ecosystems, fundraising strategy, investor relations, startup operations, and innovation management.

This hybrid, full-time role is ideal for individuals with experience supporting startups, venture capital initiatives, accelerator programs, or investor engagement activities. The position provides an opportunity to work directly with Yale innovators, investors, accelerator directors, and business development teams to strengthen Yale’s early-stage funding ecosystem.

With a competitive salary ranging from $68,000 to $120,500 annually, this opportunity offers professionals a chance to contribute to cutting-edge venture development while building a strong career within a globally recognized university environment.

About Yale Ventures

Yale Ventures serves as Yale University’s innovation and venture development platform. It helps researchers, innovators, startups, and entrepreneurs translate academic discoveries into impactful ventures and scalable solutions.

The organization works at the intersection of:

  • Academic research
  • Startup acceleration
  • Venture capital
  • Innovation ecosystems
  • Commercialization of research
  • Investor engagement
  • Entrepreneurship support

The Investor Network role is designed to strengthen Yale’s connection with early-stage investors and improve the fundraising readiness of promising ventures emerging from Yale’s ecosystem.

Overview of the Yale Ventures Investor Network Position

The selected candidate will support the development and execution of Yale Ventures’ Investor Network strategy. This includes helping ventures prepare for fundraising, coordinating investor engagement activities, managing investor intelligence systems, and ensuring effective communication between Yale innovators and investors.

The role requires someone who is highly analytical, collaborative, organized, and capable of operating in a fast-paced innovation environment.

Professionals with backgrounds in venture capital, startup operations, accelerator programs, innovation ecosystems, investor relations, or business development are strongly encouraged to apply.

Key Responsibilities of the Role

1. Early Fundraising Strategy Support

The selected candidate will work closely with accelerator directors and senior staff to support fundraising activities for Yale-affiliated ventures.

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting early fundraising strategy development
  • Preparing investor target lists
  • Conducting investor research
  • Developing background summaries for ventures
  • Matching startups with appropriate investor audiences
  • Aligning investor outreach with venture stage and capital needs

This aspect of the role is particularly valuable for professionals seeking experience in startup financing and venture growth strategy.

2. Investor Intelligence and CRM Management

The role also focuses heavily on maintaining investor intelligence systems and CRM databases.

Responsibilities include:

  • Building structured investor profiles
  • Tracking investment preferences and sector interests
  • Managing engagement histories
  • Documenting investor interactions
  • Maintaining CRM systems such as Salesforce
  • Supporting dashboard and reporting development
  • Monitoring fundraising progress metrics

Strong attention to detail and data accuracy are essential in this component of the position.

3. Investor Meeting Coordination and Preparation

The selected professional will support investor engagement activities by preparing detailed briefing materials and coordinating meetings.

Tasks include:

  • Preparing investor meeting summaries
  • Researching comparable ventures and investors
  • Coordinating logistics for meetings
  • Supporting continuity across investor interactions
  • Capturing institutional knowledge during investor discussions

This offers practical exposure to venture networking and relationship-building processes.

4. Post-Meeting Follow-Up and Investor Communication

The role also includes ongoing follow-up coordination after investor meetings.

Responsibilities include:

  • Tracking next steps after meetings
  • Supporting communication with investors
  • Coordinating internal follow-up actions
  • Ensuring consistent documentation of outcomes
  • Supporting long-term investor relationship management

These activities help ensure Yale Ventures maintains strong and organized engagement with the funding ecosystem.

5. Cross-Team Collaboration

The successful candidate will collaborate with multiple internal and external stakeholders, including:

  • Faculty Innovation teams
  • Accelerator directors
  • Business development professionals
  • External consultants
  • Venture ecosystem partners

The role requires the ability to translate between technical, academic, and business audiences while aligning investor engagement with broader venture development goals.

Required Skills and Qualifications

Applicants should possess strong professional experience and technical competencies related to innovation, fundraising, and venture ecosystems.

Required Skills

Candidates should demonstrate:

  1. Experience supporting:
    • Fundraising
    • Investor relations
    • Venture capital activities
    • Startup operations
    • Business development
  2. Strong:
    • Research skills
    • Analytical thinking
    • Organizational abilities
  3. Experience using:
    • CRM systems
    • Salesforce
    • Data management platforms
  4. Proficiency in:
    • Microsoft Excel
    • PowerPoint
    • Reporting and dashboard tools
  5. Excellent:
    • Written communication skills
    • Verbal communication abilities
    • Stakeholder coordination skills
  6. Ability to:
    • Manage multiple priorities
    • Handle confidential information professionally
    • Operate effectively in fast-paced environments

Preferred Qualifications

While not mandatory, Yale prefers candidates who have:

  • Experience within startup ecosystems
  • Exposure to venture capital firms
  • Knowledge of early-stage venture development
  • Familiarity with fundraising processes
  • Experience with PitchBook or Crunchbase
  • Strong workflow management experience
  • Ability to synthesize market and company information

Educational Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • A Bachelor’s degree in:
    • Business
    • Science
    • Engineering
    • Or a related field

Additionally, candidates must have:

  • At least four years of relevant professional experience in:
    • Venture programs
    • Startup operations
    • Innovation ecosystems
    • Investor relations
    • Related fields

Salary and Employment Benefits

One of the strongest advantages of this opportunity is the competitive compensation package.

Salary Range

  • $68,000 – $120,500 annually

Salary offers are determined based on:

  • Qualifications
  • Skills
  • Professional experience
  • Education level
  • Market conditions

Work Structure

This opportunity is offered as:

  • Full-time employment
  • Hybrid work model
  • Staff position
  • Professional category role

The hybrid structure provides flexibility while allowing employees to engage directly with Yale’s innovation ecosystem in New Haven, Connecticut.

Why This Yale Opportunity Stands Out

This position offers several important career advantages:

1. Access to a World-Class Innovation Ecosystem

Employees gain exposure to:

  • University-based innovation
  • Venture creation
  • Startup acceleration
  • Investor ecosystems
  • Academic entrepreneurship

2. Strong Professional Networking Opportunities

Professionals will interact with:

  • Investors
  • Startup founders
  • Researchers
  • Faculty innovators
  • Business leaders
  • Venture ecosystem partners

3. Career Growth in Venture Development

The role provides hands-on experience in:

  • Fundraising strategy
  • Investor engagement
  • Venture intelligence
  • Startup ecosystem management
  • CRM and analytics systems

4. Competitive Compensation and Benefits

Yale University provides robust employee support systems, wellness benefits, and professional development opportunities.

Background Check and Employment Conditions

All candidates will undergo pre-employment background screening depending on the role requirements.

These may include:

  • Background verification
  • Drug testing
  • Credit checks
  • Motor vehicle checks
  • DOT certification checks

Employment offers remain contingent upon successful completion of these requirements.

Yale University’s Commitment to Inclusion

Yale University emphasizes diversity, equity, inclusion, and equal opportunity in employment and education.

The university welcomes qualified candidates from diverse backgrounds and perspectives and does not discriminate based on:

  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • Gender identity
  • Sexual orientation
  • Religion
  • Disability
  • Veteran status
  • National origin
  • Age

Yale also maintains a tobacco-free campus environment.

Job Posting Information

  • Job Posting Date: May 14, 2026
  • Work Model: Hybrid
  • Time Type: Full-Time
  • Job Category: Professional
  • Compensation Grade: Administration & Operations
  • Duration Type: Staff Position

Application Update Process

After applying, candidates will receive a personalized link via email that allows them to:

  • Track their application status
  • Monitor important updates
  • Follow recruitment progress in real time

This personalized tracking system helps applicants stay informed throughout the recruitment process.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply directly through the official Yale careers platform.

Visit: Yale Careers Official Website

Learn more about Yale University: Yale University Official Website

 

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here  
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Yale Ventures Investor Network Role at Yale University 2026: Hybrid Full-Time Opportunity in Startup Ecosystems, Venture Capital, and Innovation Strategy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Sustainable Future Foundation: CHF 50,000 Collaborative Impact Partnership Grant 2026
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Sustainable Future Foundation has opened applications for its Collaborative Impact Partnership Programme 2026, a highly focused funding opportunity designed to support one high-impact project per year. This is not just a grant programme—it is a hands-on strategic partnership model that combines financial support with ongoing collaboration, technical guidance, and implementation support to help organisations […]

The post Sustainable Future Foundation: CHF 50,000 Collaborative Impact Partnership Grant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Sustainable Future Foundation has opened applications for its Collaborative Impact Partnership Programme 2026, a highly focused funding opportunity designed to support one high-impact project per year.

This is not just a grant programme—it is a hands-on strategic partnership model that combines financial support with ongoing collaboration, technical guidance, and implementation support to help organisations deliver measurable social and environmental impact.

With a total funding value of CHF 50,000, the programme prioritises projects that are locally led, community-driven, and aligned with global sustainability priorities.

Overview of the Collaborative Impact Partnership Programme

The Sustainable Future Collaborative Impact Partnership is designed to support one selected organisation or initiative each year to implement a high-potential project.

Unlike traditional grant programmes that only provide funding, this initiative combines:

  • Financial support
  • Strategic advisory input
  • Continuous project engagement
  • Monitoring and learning support
  • Partnership-based implementation improvement

The selected organisation remains fully responsible for day-to-day execution, while the Foundation provides structured support to strengthen planning, delivery, and long-term outcomes.

Programme Objective

The core goal of the programme is to ensure that promising sustainability projects are not only funded but are also effectively implemented and optimised for impact.

The partnership focuses on:

  • Strengthening project delivery systems
  • Improving operational effectiveness
  • Supporting adaptive learning throughout implementation
  • Enhancing long-term sustainability outcomes
  • Building stronger accountability and impact measurement

Funding Value and Support Package

The selected partner will receive:

CHF 50,000 Project Grant

  • Funding allocated to support one year of implementation
  • Flexible support for operational, programmatic, or delivery costs depending on project needs

Strategic Partnership Support

  • Regular check-ins with the Foundation team
  • Monthly project review meetings
  • Guidance on project delivery and improvement strategies
  • External perspective to strengthen decision-making

Continuous Learning & Adaptation

  • Ongoing monitoring of project progress
  • Space for reflection and improvement
  • Adjustments based on real-time implementation learning

Communications & Visibility Support

  • Opportunities for visibility through Foundation platforms
  • Support in sharing project progress and impact outcomes

Focus Areas (Priority SDGs)

The programme prioritises projects aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, especially:

  • Quality Education (SDG 4)
  • Clean Water and Sanitation (SDG 6)
  • Climate Action (SDG 13)
  • Life on Land (SDG 15)

However, applications addressing other SDGs are also welcomed if they demonstrate strong social or environmental impact.

Programme Structure and Timeline

1. Open Call for Applications

  • Applications are currently open
  • Organisations must submit a structured proposal outlining their project

2. Application Deadline

  • June 8, 2026

3. Shortlisting Phase

  • Written applications reviewed after closing date
  • Shortlisted organisations announced by end of June 2026

4. Final Presentations

  • Shortlisted candidates present project proposals
  • Online meetings held in early to mid-July 2026

5. Final Selection

  • One organisation selected by end of July 2026
  • Funding and partnership formally awarded

What Makes This Programme Unique

This initiative is designed for organisations that need more than funding alone.

Key distinguishing features include:

  • One focused annual partnership (not multiple grants)
  • Deep strategic engagement with selected organisation
  • Strong emphasis on real-world implementation success
  • Flexibility for co-funding with other donors
  • Support for both early-stage and established projects
  • Commitment to locally led development

Eligibility Criteria

Organisational Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be a non-profit organisation, NGO, or community-based initiative
  • Operate in a low- or middle-income country
  • Implement the project locally within the target community
  • Be legally non-profit (for-profit organisations are not eligible)
  • Demonstrate strong capacity for project implementation
  • Present a clear environmental or social impact focus

Additional Requirements

  • Projects must NOT be implemented in high-income countries
  • Organisations must demonstrate operational readiness and accountability
  • Co-funded projects are allowed
  • Both early-stage and mature projects are eligible

Deadline

The application deadline for the Collaborative Impact Partnership Programme is:

June 8, 2026

Late applications will not be considered, and shortlisted candidates will be contacted after review.

Click here for more and to apply

Conclusion

The Sustainable Future Collaborative Impact Partnership represents a rare opportunity for mission-driven organisations to receive not only financial support but also hands-on strategic partnership designed to strengthen real-world impact.

By combining funding with active collaboration, the programme aims to help organisations deliver stronger outcomes in education, climate action, water access, biodiversity protection, and broader sustainability efforts across the Global South.

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post Sustainable Future Foundation: CHF 50,000 Collaborative Impact Partnership Grant 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Legal Fellowship 2026 for BRAC Social Empowerment and Legal Protection Programme: Apply Now for 20 Fellowship Positions
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Are you a young legal professional looking to gain practical experience in legal aid, Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR), and case management? The BRAC Social Empowerment and Legal Protection (SELP) Programme has officially opened applications for the Legal Fellowship 2026. This 12-month fellowship offers an excellent opportunity for aspiring lawyers to work closely with experienced legal […]

The post Legal Fellowship 2026 for BRAC Social Empowerment and Legal Protection Programme: Apply Now for 20 Fellowship Positions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a young legal professional looking to gain practical experience in legal aid, Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR), and case management? The BRAC Social Empowerment and Legal Protection (SELP) Programme has officially opened applications for the Legal Fellowship 2026.

This 12-month fellowship offers an excellent opportunity for aspiring lawyers to work closely with experienced legal practitioners, contribute to access to justice initiatives, and strengthen their practical legal skills while supporting vulnerable communities across Bangladesh.

About the BRAC SELP Legal Fellowship 2026

The Legal Fellowship 2026 is designed to provide first-hand field experience for young advocates interested in legal empowerment, legal aid systems, and dispute resolution mechanisms.

Selected fellows will work directly with BRAC’s panel lawyers and legal teams to support case management processes and promote justice at the grassroots level.

Fellowship Details

  • Position: Legal Fellowship
  • Programme: BRAC Social Empowerment and Legal Protection (SELP)
  • Duration: 12 months
  • Location: Outside Dhaka, Bangladesh
  • Number of Positions: 20
  • Application Deadline: 8 June 2026

Scope of Work

Successful fellows will:

  • Work under a 12-month contractual engagement at BRAC field offices
  • Gain practical exposure to Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR)
  • Learn about Bangladesh’s legal aid systems and case management processes
  • Collaborate with BRAC panel lawyers and legal professionals
  • Receive supervision and detailed Terms of Reference (TOR) from the SELP programme
  • Obtain an official completion certificate from the SELP programme upon successful completion of the fellowship

This fellowship is ideal for young lawyers seeking hands-on legal experience in community justice and social empowerment initiatives.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Educational Qualification

  • Bachelor of Laws (LL.B) degree from a reputed university

Professional Requirements

  • Affiliation with a BAR Association as an advocate
  • Minimum one year of legal practice in an affiliated BAR is preferred but not mandatory

Required Application Documents

Interested candidates must submit:

  • A complete CV highlighting educational background and relevant legal experience
  • Contact details of two referees
  • Copies of all academic certificates
  • Copies of professional certificates

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their application documents before the deadline.

Why You Should Apply

The BRAC SELP Legal Fellowship 2026 provides a valuable platform for early-career legal professionals to:

  • Build practical legal skills
  • Gain experience in legal aid and ADR systems
  • Work on real case management processes
  • Expand professional networks in the legal sector
  • Contribute to justice and social empowerment initiatives in Bangladesh

For aspiring advocates passionate about legal empowerment and community justice, this fellowship can serve as an important stepping stone in building a meaningful legal career.

About BRAC

BRAC is one of the world’s largest international development organizations, working across multiple sectors including education, healthcare, livelihoods, gender justice, and legal empowerment. Through its Social Empowerment and Legal Protection (SELP) Programme, BRAC supports access to justice and legal protection for vulnerable populations across Bangladesh.

Application Deadline

📅 Last Date to Submit Application: 8 June 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their application documents before the deadline.

The post Legal Fellowship 2026 for BRAC Social Empowerment and Legal Protection Programme: Apply Now for 20 Fellowship Positions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR & ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Local and national NGOs working in humanitarian settings now have a major opportunity to secure funding for community protection and awareness initiatives through the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026. Jointly launched by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) and the International Council of Voluntary Agencies (ICVA), this initiative is designed […]

The post Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR & ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Local and national NGOs working in humanitarian settings now have a major opportunity to secure funding for community protection and awareness initiatives through the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026. Jointly launched by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) and the International Council of Voluntary Agencies (ICVA), this initiative is designed to strengthen grassroots efforts aimed at preventing sexual exploitation and abuse (PSEA) in vulnerable communities.

The 2026 Call for Proposals is officially open, providing grants for organizations that are developing community-centered outreach campaigns, awareness materials, communication strategies, and systems-strengthening activities tailored to local realities, languages, and cultures.

This funding opportunity is particularly important for local humanitarian actors, grassroots organizations, and civil society groups that are directly engaged with displaced populations, refugees, conflict-affected communities, and other vulnerable groups in humanitarian crises.

About the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund

The Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund was established in 2020 as a collaborative initiative between ICVA and UNHCR. Since its launch, the Fund has focused on empowering local organizations to lead awareness and prevention efforts against sexual exploitation and abuse within humanitarian settings.

The Fund recognizes that local organizations are often best positioned to understand community realities, communicate effectively in local languages, and design culturally appropriate interventions that genuinely resonate with affected populations.

The initiative aims to ensure that people receiving humanitarian assistance:

  • Understand their rights
  • Know that humanitarian aid is never conditional on favors or exchanges
  • Have access to safe reporting channels
  • Are aware of available support systems
  • Can participate in community-led protection mechanisms

By supporting local leadership, the Fund contributes to a more accountable, transparent, and community-driven humanitarian response system.

What Does the Fund Support?

The 2026 PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund supports a wide range of locally driven initiatives that improve awareness, communication, and protection systems related to sexual exploitation and abuse prevention.

1. Community-Led Protection Initiatives

The Fund prioritizes projects that place communities at the center of protection efforts. Organizations are encouraged to involve affected populations directly in the design and implementation of outreach strategies.

Examples include:

  • Community awareness campaigns
  • Peer-to-peer education initiatives
  • Grassroots advocacy programs
  • Survivor-centered communication approaches
  • Community dialogue sessions

2. Locally Tailored Communication Materials

Supported projects are expected to create outreach materials adapted to:

  • Local languages
  • Literacy levels
  • Cultural realities
  • Vulnerable population groups
  • Indigenous communication systems

Materials may include:

  • Posters
  • Radio programs
  • Comics and illustrations
  • Community theatre
  • Videos
  • Social media campaigns
  • Audio messaging
  • Educational toolkits

3. Strengthening Local Leadership in PSEA

The Fund is committed to strengthening local ownership of PSEA initiatives and supporting grassroots organizations in becoming leaders within humanitarian protection systems.

This aligns with major international commitments including:

  • The Grand Bargain localization agenda
  • The Global Compact on Refugees
  • Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) localization commitments

4. Open-Source PSEA Materials and Knowledge Sharing

All outreach materials created through funded projects become part of a publicly accessible deliverables database. This allows organizations globally to adapt and reuse effective communication tools while reducing duplication and improving consistency in humanitarian messaging.

Impact of the PSEA Outreach Fund

Since its establishment, the Fund has already demonstrated measurable results across multiple humanitarian contexts worldwide.

Key Achievements

65+ PSEA Outreach Projects Funded

The initiative has already financed more than 65 grassroots protection and awareness projects implemented by local organizations.

Over 2 Million People Reached

Fund-supported projects have collectively reached more than 2 million people with awareness messaging and protection information.

Outreach Materials Produced in 60+ Languages

Projects have successfully developed culturally adapted communication materials in more than 60 languages, significantly improving accessibility and inclusivity.

More Than 450 Outreach Materials Developed

Organizations have produced hundreds of educational and awareness materials that are now available for adaptation and reuse globally.

Independent Evaluation Findings

An independent evaluation supported by the UK Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) confirmed the strong impact and strategic importance of the Fund.

Major Findings Include:

84% Increase in Community Awareness

Organizations reported a dramatic increase in awareness related to sexual exploitation and abuse prevention across communities reached.

Increased Reporting Mechanisms

Approximately 85% of participating organizations observed moderate or significant increases in reporting of SEA-related incidents after project implementation.

Stronger Local Organizations

78% of organizations stated that implementing a Fund-supported project significantly strengthened their internal PSEA capacity.

Community Participation Matters

68% of respondents indicated that communities were heavily involved in project design and implementation, increasing trust and effectiveness.

Why This Fund Matters in Humanitarian Settings

Sexual exploitation and abuse remain major concerns in humanitarian contexts where vulnerable populations often face heightened risks and power imbalances.

The PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund addresses these challenges by:

  • Supporting localized protection strategies
  • Increasing awareness among vulnerable groups
  • Promoting safe reporting systems
  • Strengthening accountability
  • Encouraging survivor-centered approaches
  • Building trust between aid providers and communities

Importantly, the Fund moves beyond simple awareness campaigns by encouraging projects that address structural issues and power dynamics that contribute to exploitation and abuse.

Eligibility and Who Can Apply

The 2026 Call for Proposals is primarily aimed at local and national NGOs responding to humanitarian crises.

Organizations applying should demonstrate:

  • Community engagement experience
  • Capacity to implement outreach activities
  • Knowledge of local contexts and languages
  • Commitment to protection and safeguarding principles
  • Ability to develop culturally appropriate communication materials

Projects should focus on humanitarian settings and prioritize at-risk populations.

Application Timeline

Applications Opened:  19 May 2026

Application Deadline:  14 June 2026 at 23:59 CET

Late applications may not be considered, so organizations are encouraged to begin preparing proposals early.

Available Resources for Applicants

Applicants can access guidance and application materials in multiple languages, including:

  • English
  • Arabic
  • French

The Fund also provides access to:

  • PSEA materials database
  • Project summaries from previous funding rounds
  • ICVA PSEAH publications
  • Application guidance documents

Benefits of Participating in the Fund

Organizations selected for funding can benefit from:

  1. Financial support for PSEA outreach projects
  2. Increased organizational visibility
  3. Capacity strengthening opportunities
  4. Access to global PSEA resources
  5. Participation in international humanitarian protection networks
  6. Contribution to community-led safeguarding systems
  7. Opportunities for long-term partnership development

How to Apply

Interested organizations should carefully review the official guidance documents and application materials before submitting proposals.

Applicants are advised to:

  1. Read all application guidance thoroughly
  2. Clearly define community needs
  3. Demonstrate local engagement
  4. Explain sustainability plans
  5. Highlight culturally adapted approaches
  6. Include measurable outcomes and indicators

Apply, Click Here

For more information about the proposals, visit the official website

Final Thoughts

The Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026 represents a significant opportunity for grassroots organizations and humanitarian actors to strengthen protection systems and empower vulnerable communities.

By supporting locally led initiatives, UNHCR and ICVA are reinforcing the importance of community ownership, accountability, and culturally grounded communication in preventing sexual exploitation and abuse in humanitarian contexts.

Organizations with innovative, community-centered outreach ideas are strongly encouraged to apply before the June 14, 2026 deadline.

Opportunity Category

Grant

Eligible Applicants

  • Local NGOs
  • National NGOs
  • Civil Society Organizations (CSOs)
  • Humanitarian organizations
  • Community-led organizations
  • Grassroots protection groups

Eligible Regions

Open to organizations operating in humanitarian settings globally.

Required Qualifications

  • Experience in humanitarian or protection-related work
  • Capacity to implement community outreach activities
  • Understanding of safeguarding and PSEA principles
  • Ability to develop localized communication materials
  • Strong community engagement approach

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

 

The post Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR & ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Bologna PhD in Political and Social Sciences 2026/2027 in Italy: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunity for International Students
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The University of Bologna has officially opened applications for its prestigious PhD in Political and Social Sciences for the 2026/2027 academic year. This internationally recognized doctoral programme offers aspiring researchers and academics the opportunity to pursue advanced studies in one of Europe’s oldest and most respected universities. The four-year doctoral programme is designed for students […]

The post University of Bologna PhD in Political and Social Sciences 2026/2027 in Italy: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Bologna has officially opened applications for its prestigious PhD in Political and Social Sciences for the 2026/2027 academic year. This internationally recognized doctoral programme offers aspiring researchers and academics the opportunity to pursue advanced studies in one of Europe’s oldest and most respected universities.

The four-year doctoral programme is designed for students interested in political science, sociology, social policy, governance, public administration, economic policy, and broader social science research. Conducted entirely in English, the programme provides an outstanding academic environment for candidates seeking interdisciplinary research opportunities and international academic exposure.

Applications for the second round close on 15 June 2026 at 11:59 PM.

About the University of Bologna

Founded in 1088, the University of Bologna is widely regarded as the oldest university in the Western world. Known internationally for its academic excellence, innovative research culture, and global partnerships, the university consistently attracts students and researchers from across the globe.

The institution is particularly recognized for:

  • Excellence in social sciences and humanities
  • Strong internationalization policies
  • Interdisciplinary research opportunities
  • Extensive mobility and exchange programmes
  • Commitment to innovation and societal impact

Its PhD programmes are designed to produce highly skilled researchers capable of contributing to academia, policymaking, public institutions, NGOs, and international organizations.

Overview of the PhD in Political and Social Sciences

The PhD in Political and Social Sciences is part of the university’s subject area in Legal, Politological, Economic, and Statistical Sciences.

Key Programme Details

  • Institution: University of Bologna
  • Programme: PhD in Political and Social Sciences
  • Academic Year: 2026/2027
  • Cycle: 42
  • Duration: 4 Years
  • Language of Instruction: English
  • Location: Bologna, Italy
  • Programme Start Date: 1 November 2026
  • Coordinator: Roberto Rizza

The programme combines rigorous theoretical foundations with advanced empirical and interdisciplinary research approaches.

Scholarship and Financial Benefits

One of the biggest advantages of this doctoral programme is the financial support provided to selected candidates.

Scholarship Value

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Approximately €1,350 net per month
  • Financial support throughout the programme duration
  • Potential adjustments depending on professional pension contributions or external employment income

The scholarship is intended to support doctoral candidates with living expenses while allowing them to fully focus on research activities and academic development.

International Mobility Opportunity

The programme also includes an international mobility component, enabling doctoral candidates to spend up to six months abroad during their studies.

This mobility opportunity helps students:

  • Build international academic collaborations
  • Access global research networks
  • Conduct fieldwork or research abroad
  • Enhance cross-cultural academic experience
  • Strengthen future career opportunities

International mobility is considered a core component of the programme’s research training philosophy.

Research Areas and Academic Focus

The PhD programme encourages innovative and interdisciplinary research within political and social sciences.

Possible areas of study include:

Political Science

  • Democracy and governance
  • Comparative politics
  • Political participation
  • Public policy analysis
  • International relations

Sociology and Social Policy

  • Social inequalities
  • Welfare systems
  • Migration studies
  • Labour markets
  • Social transformation

Economic and Statistical Analysis

  • Political economy
  • Quantitative social research
  • Data analysis in social sciences
  • Economic governance

Public Administration and Institutions

  • Institutional reforms
  • Public sector governance
  • Policy implementation
  • Administrative systems

Students are encouraged to develop original research projects that contribute meaningfully to current academic and societal debates.

Why Study Political and Social Sciences at the University of Bologna?

Choosing this programme offers several academic and professional advantages.

1. World-Class Academic Reputation

The University of Bologna has a long-standing global reputation for academic excellence and research innovation.

2. English-Taught Doctoral Programme

The programme is fully taught in English, making it accessible to international students from diverse regions.

3. Strong Research Environment

Doctoral candidates work alongside experienced faculty members and international scholars within a dynamic research community.

4. Interdisciplinary Learning

Students benefit from collaboration across disciplines including political science, economics, sociology, law, and statistics.

5. Career Development Opportunities

Graduates may pursue careers in:

  • Universities and academia
  • International organizations
  • Government agencies
  • NGOs
  • Think tanks
  • Research institutions
  • Public policy consulting

Application Deadline

Second Round Application Deadline:

15 June 2026 at 11:59 PM

Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparing their documents early to avoid last-minute submission challenges.

Required Documents

Applicants may be required to submit the following:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Academic transcripts
  3. Degree certificates
  4. Research proposal or statement of purpose
  5. Identification documents
  6. Additional programme-specific forms

The university also provides an official Curriculum Vitae form as part of the application process.

Admission and Selection Process

Admission to the programme is highly competitive and based on academic excellence and research potential.

Selection criteria may include:

  • Academic background and performance
  • Research experience
  • Quality of research proposal
  • Alignment with programme research areas
  • Motivation and future academic goals

Candidates should carefully review the official call for applications and programme table before submitting their applications.

Living and Studying in Bologna, Italy

Bologna is one of Italy’s most vibrant student cities and offers a rich academic and cultural experience.

Students studying in Bologna can enjoy:

  • Historic academic atmosphere
  • International student community
  • Affordable student lifestyle compared to many European cities
  • Excellent public transportation
  • Rich cultural and culinary traditions
  • Strong research and innovation ecosystem

The city is considered one of Europe’s best destinations for higher education and student life.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official University of Bologna PhD application portal before the deadline.

Applicants are advised to:

  1. Read the official call carefully
  2. Prepare all required documents
  3. Develop a strong research proposal
  4. Ensure English proficiency where required
  5. Submit applications before the deadline

Learn more and apply, click here

Final Thoughts

The University of Bologna PhD in Political and Social Sciences 2026/2027 is an exceptional opportunity for students and researchers passionate about understanding and shaping political and social transformations globally.

With generous financial support, international mobility opportunities, interdisciplinary research exposure, and access to one of Europe’s leading universities, this programme provides a strong platform for future academic and professional success.

Prospective applicants interested in political science, governance, sociology, public policy, and social research should strongly consider applying before the June 15, 2026 deadline.

Eligible Applicants

  • International students
  • Domestic students
  • Graduates in political science and social sciences
  • Researchers interested in interdisciplinary social science research

Eligible Countries/Regions

Open to applicants worldwide.

Required Qualifications

  • Master’s degree or equivalent qualification
  • Strong academic background in political science, sociology, economics, statistics, law, or related disciplines
  • Research interest in political and social sciences
  • English language proficiency
  • Strong research and analytical skills

 

Discover more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post University of Bologna PhD in Political and Social Sciences 2026/2027 in Italy: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunity for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Barr Foundation has officially reopened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Barr Fellowship Program, a transformative leadership initiative designed to support nonprofit leaders navigating today’s increasingly demanding social impact landscape. After a strategic review and relaunch, the Barr Fellowship is returning with a renewed vision focused on resilience, renewal, leadership sustainability, collaboration, and relationship-building […]

The post Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Barr Foundation has officially reopened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Barr Fellowship Program, a transformative leadership initiative designed to support nonprofit leaders navigating today’s increasingly demanding social impact landscape.

After a strategic review and relaunch, the Barr Fellowship is returning with a renewed vision focused on resilience, renewal, leadership sustainability, collaboration, and relationship-building among nonprofit leaders across Massachusetts.

The Fellowship recognizes extraordinary civic and nonprofit leaders while providing substantial financial support, executive coaching, wellness resources, and long-term networking opportunities that strengthen both individuals and organizations.

Applications for the 2026 Barr Fellows cohort are now open.

About the Barr Fellowship

The Barr Fellowship was first launched in 2005 by the Barr Foundation to celebrate and strengthen exceptional leadership across the nonprofit and social impact sectors.

Over the years, the Fellowship has supported more than 80 leaders across seven cohorts, creating a vibrant network of changemakers working in different fields and communities throughout Massachusetts.

Following the challenges brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, racial justice movements, increasing climate concerns, and growing pressure on nonprofit organizations, the Foundation conducted an extensive review of the Fellowship program.

The result is a refreshed Fellowship experience built specifically to help leaders:

  • Combat burnout and isolation
  • Improve resilience and sustainability
  • Develop collaborative leadership skills
  • Build stronger professional networks
  • Foster healthier organizational cultures
  • Invest in personal well-being and renewal

The 2026 Fellowship reflects a growing recognition that nonprofit leaders need more than funding alone — they also need support systems, trusted relationships, wellness opportunities, and sustainable leadership practices.

What the Barr Fellowship 2026 Offers

Selected Fellows and their organizations will receive a comprehensive package of financial, professional, and leadership support over a 15-month period.

1. $75,000 Organizational Grant

Each selected Fellow’s organization will receive a total grant of $75,000, divided into two major components:

  • $25,000 dedicated to the Fellow’s personal well-being, leadership development, and renewal
  • $50,000 allocated to organizational resilience, staff wellness, and operational sustainability

This funding structure ensures that both the individual leader and their organization benefit from the Fellowship experience.

2. Executive and Organizational Coaching

Participants will receive 50 hours of coaching support, including:

  • Executive coaching for the Fellow
  • Organizational coaching focused on resilience-building and leadership sustainability
  • Strategic guidance to strengthen organizational culture and collaborative leadership practices

The coaching component is designed to help leaders navigate complex challenges while developing healthier and more effective leadership approaches.

3. Cohort Learning and Leadership Development

Fellows will participate in approximately 16 days of scheduled programming over 15 months.

The program includes:

  • Leadership retreats
  • Peer learning opportunities
  • Relationship-building sessions
  • Collaborative leadership development
  • Networking experiences across sectors

The cohort model allows Fellows to connect deeply with leaders from diverse backgrounds and disciplines across Massachusetts.

4. Lifelong Alumni Network

After completing the program, participants become part of the broader Barr Fellows Network.

This alumni network provides ongoing:

  • Collaboration opportunities
  • Peer support
  • Leadership exchange
  • Strategic partnerships
  • Community engagement opportunities

The network aims to strengthen long-term collaboration among social impact leaders across the Commonwealth.

Program Goals of the Barr Fellowship

The Barr Fellowship seeks to empower nonprofit leaders to:

Increase Personal and Professional Resilience

The program prioritizes leader wellness, helping participants cultivate sustainability, clarity, and renewal in demanding leadership roles.

Strengthen Collaborative Leadership Skills

Fellows gain tools and experiences that improve teamwork, partnership-building, and cross-sector collaboration.

Build Healthy Organizational Cultures

The Fellowship encourages organizations to create environments where staff can thrive, contribute meaningfully, and feel supported.

Expand Leadership Networks

Participants build valuable relationships that foster mutual aid, solidarity, innovation, and long-term social impact collaboration.

Why the Fellowship Matters Today

Nonprofit organizations continue to face immense pressure due to:

  • Reduced resources
  • Increased community needs
  • Workforce burnout
  • Economic uncertainty
  • Social justice challenges
  • Climate-related concerns
  • DEI backlash and organizational stress

The Barr Foundation recognizes that leadership in the social sector can often become isolating and unsustainable without intentional support systems.

The Fellowship was redesigned specifically to address these realities by helping leaders prioritize wellness, resilience, connection, and organizational sustainability.

Eligibility Criteria for the Barr Fellowship 2026

To qualify for the Fellowship, both the organization and the nominated leader must meet specific eligibility requirements.

Organizational Eligibility Requirements

Applicant organizations must:

  • Be a nonprofit 501(c)(3) public charity or fiscally sponsored by one
  • Be headquartered in Massachusetts
  • Conduct at least 75% of activities or programming in Massachusetts
  • Have an operating budget between approximately $500,000 and $5 million
  • Demonstrate strong commitment to justice, belonging, and inclusive leadership
  • Maintain adequate staffing and operational capacity during Fellowship participation

Organizations That Are Not Eligible

The following organizations cannot apply:

  • Government agencies
  • Hospitals
  • K-12 schools
  • Higher education institutions
  • Organizations primarily focused on re-granting funds

Individual Eligibility Requirements

The organization’s nominated candidate must:

  • Serve as CEO, Executive Director, or Co-Director
  • Have held their current role for at least 3 years
  • Have 3–10 years of nonprofit executive leadership experience
  • Be a full-time resident of New England
  • Work professionally with significant focus on Massachusetts
  • Demonstrate commitment to justice, belonging, and healthy organizational culture
  • Commit fully to all Fellowship activities and participation requirements

Barr Fellowship 2026 Application Timeline

Applicants should carefully note the following important dates:

Key Dates

  • Application Opens: May 14, 2026
  • Information Session 1: June 11, 2026 (3–4 PM ET)
  • Information Session 2: June 16, 2026 (1–2 PM ET)
  • Application Deadline: June 30, 2026
  • Advancing Candidates Notification: August 2026
  • Candidate Calls: August 2026
  • Finalist Notifications: Early September 2026
  • Finalist Interviews: Late September to Early October 2026
  • Cohort and Award Announcement: November 2026

Deadline

The application deadline for the Barr Fellowship 2026 is June 30, 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early and attend one of the scheduled information sessions to better understand the Fellowship expectations and selection process.

How to Apply

Click here for more and to apply

Before applying, candidates should carefully review all eligibility requirements and ensure they can fully participate in the Fellowship’s programming and leadership commitments.

The Barr Fellowship represents a unique opportunity for nonprofit leaders to invest in personal renewal, strengthen organizational culture, expand collaborative leadership capacity, and join a powerful network of social impact leaders across Massachusetts.

For leaders committed to sustainable change and community impact, this Fellowship offers both transformational support and long-term professional connection opportunities.

Click here for more global fellowship opportunities

The post Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IGAD Media Awards 2026 in Uganda: Call for Journalists, Digital Creators, and Storytellers Reporting on Migration, Mobility, and Regional Integration
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Intergovernmental Authority on Development has officially launched the highly anticipated IGAD Media Awards 2026, a prestigious regional recognition platform celebrating impactful journalism and digital storytelling focused on migration, labour mobility, forced displacement, regional integration, and human mobility across the IGAD region. The awards aim to honor journalists, media practitioners, digital creators, photojournalists, and storytellers […]
